     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Include conversation_message for initial message handling in downstream views
    ic.conversation_message,
    ic.conversation_message.author AS conversation_message_author
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  conversation_message,
  conversation_message_author,
  'Intercom' AS original_source
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  NULL AS conversation_message,  -- Hiver doesn't have conversation_message structure
  NULL AS conversation_message_author  -- Hiver doesn't have initial message author
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content (from either conversation_parts.body or conversation_message.body)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - Triggered by Intercom tags: "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue", "t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from both message content and support team tagging

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations
- public.view_cx_conversations: Unified conversation data with unnested tags and metadata

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'

  UNION ALL

  -- Include initial conversation messages (or placeholder) for conversations without comment parts
  -- This ensures ALL conversations are represented, even those with no messages
  SELECT
    CONCAT('conv_', c.id) AS part_id,  -- Create unique ID for initial message
    COALESCE(c.conversation_message.body::VARCHAR, '') AS body,  -- Use empty string if NULL
    c.conversation_message_author AS author,
    c.id::BIGINT AS conversation_id,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6
  FROM public.view_cx_conversations c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 
    WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
    AND cp2.part_type = 'comment'
  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

lines: - conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
+- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' - Hiver not included as this view only processes Intercom data)

    c.original_source
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content (from either conversation_parts.body or conversation_message.body)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' - Hiver not included as this view only processes Intercom data)

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - Triggered by Intercom tags: "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue", "t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from both message content and support team tagging

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations
- public.view_cx_conversations: Unified conversation data with unnested tags and metadata

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'

  UNION ALL

  -- Include initial conversation messages (or placeholder) for conversations without comment parts
  -- This ensures ALL conversations are represented, even those with no messages
  SELECT
    CONCAT('conv_', c.id) AS part_id,  -- Create unique ID for initial message
    COALESCE(c.conversation_message.body::VARCHAR, '') AS body,  -- Use empty string if NULL
    c.conversation_message_author AS author,
    c.id::BIGINT AS conversation_id,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6
  FROM public.view_cx_conversations c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 
    WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
    AND cp2.part_type = 'comment'
  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'

  UNION ALL

  -- Include initial conversation messages (or placeholder) for conversations without comment parts
  -- This ensures ALL conversations are represented, even those with no messages
  SELECT
    CONCAT('conv_', c.id) AS part_id,  -- Create unique ID for initial message
    COALESCE(c.conversation_message.body::VARCHAR, '') AS body,  -- Use empty string if NULL
    c.conversation_message_author AS author,
    c.id::BIGINT AS conversation_id,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.view_cx_conversations c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 
    WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
    AND cp2.part_type = 'comment'
  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583,
  original_source
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

lines: - conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
-- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' - Hiver not included as this view only processes Intercom data)
+- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' only - this view filters to Intercom conversations)

content.text:
## Summary of Changes

I've added the `original_source` column to both views:

### 1. **`07_view_cx_conversations.sql`:**
   - Added `'Intercom' AS original_source` for Intercom conversations
   - Added `'Hiver' AS original_source` for Hiver conversations
   - Updated documentation to include the new column

### 2. **`08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql`:**
   - Added `c.original_source` to both parts of the UNION in the CTE
   - Added `original_source` to the final SELECT
   - Updated documentation to note this will always be 'Intercom' since the view filters to only Intercom conversations

The `original_source` column will help you easily identify whether each conversation originally came from Hiver or Intercom, which is especially useful given the migration and data consolidation between the two systems.

You can now run both SQL scripts to update the tables with the new column!

content.text:
I see the issue! The `conversation_message` column in `intercom_conversations` is completely empty (NULL for all records). The initial messages are actually stored as the first `comment` type record in `intercom_conversation_parts`. 

Since `conversation_message` is always NULL, we need to remove references to it from both views. The `08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql` view is already correctly getting the initial message from the first comment part.

Fix conversation_message extraction in view_cx_conversations
Remove conversation_message references from both views

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- conversation_message: JSON structure with initial message body and author (Intercom only)
- conversation_message_author: JSON structure with initial message author details (Intercom only)
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Include conversation_message for initial message handling in downstream views
    ic.conversation_message,
    ic.conversation_message.author AS conversation_message_author
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  conversation_message,
  conversation_message_author,
  'Intercom' AS original_source
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  NULL AS conversation_message,  -- Hiver doesn't have conversation_message structure
  NULL AS conversation_message_author,  -- Hiver doesn't have initial message author
  'Hiver' AS original_source
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

- conversation_message_author: JSON structure with initial message author details (Intercom only)
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  NULL AS conversation_message,  -- Hiver doesn't have conversation_message structure
  NULL AS conversation_message_author,  -- Hiver doesn't have initial message author
  'Hiver' AS original_source
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- conversation_message: JSON structure with initial message body and author (Intercom only)
- conversation_message_author: JSON structure with initial message author details (Intercom only)
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content (from either conversation_parts.body or conversation_message.body)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' only - this view filters to Intercom conversations)

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - Triggered by Intercom tags: "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue", "t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from both message content and support team tagging

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations
- public.view_cx_conversations: Unified conversation data with unnested tags and metadata

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'

  UNION ALL

  -- Include initial conversation messages (or placeholder) for conversations without comment parts
  -- This ensures ALL conversations are represented, even those with no messages
  SELECT
    CONCAT('conv_', c.id) AS part_id,  -- Create unique ID for initial message
    COALESCE(c.conversation_message.body::VARCHAR, '') AS body,  -- Use empty string if NULL
    c.conversation_message_author AS author,
    c.id::BIGINT AS conversation_id,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.view_cx_conversations c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 
    WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
    AND cp2.part_type = 'comment'
  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583,
  original_source
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content (from either conversation_parts.body or conversation_message.body)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' only - this view filters to Intercom conversations)

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - Triggered by Intercom tags: "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue", "t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from both message content and support team tagging

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations
- public.view_cx_conversations: Unified conversation data with unnested tags and metadata

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts

  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)

  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)

  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'

    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583,

FROM all_parts;

ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

content.text:
## Summary

The issue was that `conversation_message` in the `intercom_conversations` table is **always NULL** - it appears this column exists but was never populated with data. The actual initial messages are stored as the first `comment` type record in `intercom_conversation_parts`.

I've fixed both views by:

### 1. **`07_view_cx_conversations.sql`:**
   - Removed `conversation_message` and `conversation_message_author` columns since they're always NULL
   - Kept the `original_source` column to identify Intercom vs Hiver conversations

### 2. **`08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql`:**
   - Changed the placeholder for conversations without parts to use empty string and NULL author
   - Updated documentation to reflect that body comes from conversation_parts only

The views now correctly handle the fact that initial messages are stored in `intercom_conversation_parts` with `part_type = 'comment'`, not in the `conversation_message` column.

message.content: I don't get why you did that with the part id? doesn't each conversation part have an id?

content.text:
You're absolutely right! Each conversation part already has its own ID from the `intercom_conversation_parts` table. The `CONCAT('conv_', c.id)` was only needed for conversations that have NO conversation parts at all - it creates a placeholder record to ensure every conversation is represented in the view.

Let me clarify the logic in `08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql`:

1. **First part of UNION**: Gets all actual conversation parts with `part_type = 'comment'` - these use the real `cp.id` as `part_id`

2. **Second part of UNION**: Only creates placeholder records for conversations that have NO comment parts at all. Since these don't have a real part ID, we create a synthetic one using `CONCAT('conv_', c.id)` to ensure uniqueness.

The WHERE clause `WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT AND cp2.part_type = 'comment')` ensures we only create these placeholders for conversations with zero comment parts.

This approach ensures:
- Every conversation appears in the view at least once
- Conversations with no messages get a single placeholder row
- Conversations with messages use their actual part IDs

Does this make more sense? The synthetic ID is only for the edge case of conversations with no parts at all.

message.content: got it, makes sense!

content.text:
Great! The setup ensures complete coverage - every conversation gets represented in the view, whether it has actual message parts or not. 

The two views should now work correctly together:
- `07_view_cx_conversations` provides unified conversation-level data from both Intercom and Hiver with the new `original_source` column
- `08_intercom_conversations_with_topics` uses that as a source for topic detection, pulling in the unnested tags and handling both real conversation parts and placeholders for empty conversations

Both SQL scripts are ready to run whenever you're ready to deploy these changes!
