toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

lines: CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
+  CASE WHEN mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE mailbox_contact END AS customer_email
-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new

content.text:
You're raising a really important architectural question! Let me think through this conundrum with you:

## The Current Flow:
- `07_view_cx_conversations` → conversation-level aggregated data (tags, metrics, etc.)
- `08_intercom_conversations_with_topics` → message-level analysis (using `07` as source)

## The Challenge:
Topic detection happens at the **message level** (in `08`) because that's where we have the actual message bodies, but you might want to know at the **conversation level** (in `07`) whether ANY message in that conversation mentions certain topics.

## Your Insight is Spot On:
There's a potential mismatch where:
- A conversation might have messages mentioning "1583" in the body
- But the conversation wasn't tagged with "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue"
- This discrepancy would be valuable to surface!

## Potential Approaches:

**Option 1: Keep current architecture, add aggregation**
- Keep `08` dependent on `07`
- Add a separate query/column in `07` that checks if ANY message part contains keywords
- Show both "has_1583_tag" and "has_1583_mention_in_body" at conversation level

**Option 2: Reverse the dependency**
- Have `08` go directly to source tables (skip `07`)
- Then `07` could JOIN with `08` to get aggregated topic flags
- Downside: `08` would need to duplicate some joins/logic

**Option 3: Three-layer approach**
- Base conversation view (current `07` without topics)
- Message-level analysis (current `08`)  
- Enriched conversation view that combines both

The key insight is that **topic detection is inherently a message-level operation** that needs to be **aggregated up to conversations**. Having both "tagged as X" vs "actually mentions X" would be super valuable for quality control!

What's your preference - would you rather have the topic detection flow up from messages to conversations, or keep them as complementary views that can be joined when needed?

message.content: what's your best recommendation? I am leaning towards 1 or 3, but what is the best approach in your opinion?

content.text:
I strongly recommend **Option 3: Three-layer approach**. Here's why:

## Recommended Architecture:

1. **`07_view_cx_conversations`** - Base conversation facts
   - Keep it clean: just conversation-level data (metrics, tags, customer_email, etc.)
   - No topic detection logic here
   - Single source of truth for conversation attributes

2. **`08_intercom_conversations_with_topics`** - Message-level analysis
   - Reads from base tables directly (not from `07`)
   - Does all the heavy lifting of topic detection on message bodies
   - One row per message part

3. **`09_view_cx_conversations_enriched`** (new) - Conversation + Topics
   - JOINs `07` with aggregated data from `08`
   - Adds columns like:
     - `has_1583_in_tags` (from tags in `07`)
     - `has_1583_in_messages` (aggregated from `08`)
     - `missing_1583_tag` (messages mention it but not tagged)
     - `count_1583_mentions` (how many messages mention it)

## Why This is Best:

✅ **Separation of Concerns**: Each view has one clear job
✅ **No Circular Dependencies**: Clean data flow
✅ **Reusability**: Can use `08` for other message-level analysis without carrying conversation baggage
✅ **Quality Control**: Easy to spot tagging gaps (mentions without tags)
✅ **Performance**: Base views stay fast; enrichment only happens when needed
✅ **Maintainability**: Changes to topic detection don't require rebuilding conversation base data

## The Key Insight:
Your instinct about the "conundrum" is spot-on - topic detection and conversation attributes are fundamentally different concerns that happen at different granularities. This architecture respects that difference while giving you the best of both worlds when you need them combined.

Want me to help refactor the views this way?

message.content: in this layout, is your enriched table (the last one) one row per conversation or conversation part?

content.text:
The enriched table (`09_view_cx_conversations_enriched`) would be **one row per conversation** - it's taking the conversation-level data from `07` and enriching it with aggregated topic information from `08`.

## Example of what it would look like:

```sql
-- 09_view_cx_conversations_enriched (one row per conversation)
conversation_id | customer_email | created_at | has_1583_in_tags | has_1583_in_messages | count_1583_mentions | missing_1583_tag
123            | user@ex.com    | 2025-01-01 | false           | true                 | 3                   | true
456            | other@ex.com   | 2025-01-02 | true            | true                 | 5                   | false
789            | third@ex.com   | 2025-01-03 | false           | false                | 0                   | false
```

The enriched view would:
1. Start with all conversations from `07` (one row each)
2. LEFT JOIN with aggregated data from `08` grouped by conversation_id
3. Add computed flags showing discrepancies

This gives you the best analytical view - you can easily answer questions like:
- "How many conversations mention 1583 but aren't tagged?"
- "What's the average number of 1583 mentions per conversation?"
- "Which customers repeatedly ask about 1583?"

All at the conversation level, which is typically what you want for reporting and analysis.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content from conversation_parts (empty for conversations without parts)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' only - this view filters to Intercom conversations)

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - Triggered by Intercom tags: "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue", "t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from both message content and support team tagging

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations
- public.view_cx_conversations: Unified conversation data with unnested tags and metadata

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content from conversation_parts (empty for conversations without parts)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' only - this view filters to Intercom conversations)

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - Triggered by Intercom tags: "t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue", "t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from both message content and support team tagging

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'

  UNION ALL

  -- Include placeholder for conversations without comment parts
  -- This ensures ALL conversations are represented, even those with no messages
  SELECT
    CONCAT('conv_', c.id) AS part_id,  -- Create unique ID for placeholder
    '' AS body,  -- Empty body for conversations without parts
    NULL AS author,  -- No author for placeholder
    c.id::BIGINT AS conversation_id,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.view_cx_conversations c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 
    WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
    AND cp2.part_type = 'comment'
  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583,
  original_source
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content from conversation_parts (empty for conversations without parts)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from message content

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Include all comment parts from conversation_parts
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'

  UNION ALL

  -- Include placeholder for conversations without comment parts
  -- This ensures ALL conversations are represented, even those with no messages
  SELECT
    CONCAT('conv_', c.id) AS part_id,  -- Create unique ID for placeholder
    '' AS body,  -- Empty body for conversations without parts
    NULL AS author,  -- No author for placeholder
    c.id::BIGINT AS conversation_id,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS part_created_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT AS conversation_created_at,
    c.tag_1,
    c.tag_2,
    c.tag_3,
    c.tag_4,
    c.tag_5,
    c.tag_6,
    c.original_source
  FROM public.view_cx_conversations c
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp2 
    WHERE cp2.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT
    AND cp2.part_type = 'comment'
  )
    -- Only process Intercom conversations (not Hiver)
    AND c.id ~ '^[0-9]+$'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  -- Also checks for specific Intercom tags: t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue and t-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue'
    OR tag_1 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_2 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_3 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_4 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_5 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay'
    OR tag_6 = 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583,
  original_source
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

+  -- Each row represents one message in a conversation

-  JOIN public.view_cx_conversations c ON cp.conversation_id = c.id::BIGINT

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
PURPOSE: Track and analyze Intercom conversation topics for customer service insights
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table processes Intercom conversation data to identify key customer service topics
and patterns. Each row represents a single conversation part (message) OR initial 
conversation message with automated topic detection for 1583 forms, verification 
processes, and account types.

USE CASES:
- Monitor customer inquiries about 1583 tax forms
- Track verification-related support requests
- Analyze primary vs secondary account questions
- Filter out bot spam for cleaner analytics
- Customer service topic trending in Metabase dashboards

KEY COLUMNS:
- part_id: Unique identifier for each conversation part (or 'conv_[id]' for initial messages)
- body: The actual message text content from conversation_parts (empty for conversations without parts)
- author_type: Classification of who sent the message (user/admin/bot)
- author_email: Email address of the message sender
- conversation_id: Links to parent conversation for threading
- part_created_date: Timestamp when this specific message was created
- conversation_created_date: Timestamp when the overall conversation started
- bot_spam: TRUE if message contains "unmonitored inbox" (automated messages)
- mentions_1583: TRUE if message mentions 1583, verification, primary, or secondary

TOPIC DETECTION LOGIC:
bot_spam flag:
  - Triggered by: "%unmonitored inbox%" (case-insensitive)
  - Purpose: Filter automated/system messages from analysis

mentions_1583 flag:
  - Triggered by message content: "%1583%", "%verification%", "%primary%", "%secondary%"
  - All matches are case-insensitive using ILIKE
  - Helps identify tax form and account-related inquiries from message content

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Individual messages within conversations

DATA TRANSFORMATIONS:
- Includes BOTH conversation parts (replies) AND initial conversation messages (first emails)
- Unix timestamps converted to TIMESTAMP format using epoch calculation
- JSON author fields extracted and cleaned (removes quotes)
- Only 'comment' type parts included (excludes system messages)
- Initial conversation messages included for conversations without comment parts

FILTERING:
- Only includes conversation parts where part_type = 'comment'
- Initial messages only included if conversation has no comment parts (avoids duplicates)
- This excludes system-generated parts like assignments, notes, etc.

REFRESH STRATEGY:
- Uses atomic swap pattern (create new, swap, drop old)
- Table despite view_ prefix to avoid CASCADE dependency issues
- DISTKEY on conversation_id for optimal join performance
- SORTKEY on dates for efficient time-based queries

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation_id for efficient joins
- Sorted by creation dates for time-series analysis
- Table format provides faster query performance than view

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find all 1583-related conversations in the last 30 days
SELECT conversation_id, part_created_date, body
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  AND part_created_date >= CURRENT_DATE - 30
  AND bot_spam = FALSE
ORDER BY part_created_date DESC;

-- Count daily conversation topics
SELECT
  DATE(part_created_date) as date,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 END) as count_1583_mentions,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as total_conversations
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze author types for 1583 conversations
SELECT
  author_type,
  COUNT(*) as message_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as conversation_count
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Source table for message data
- public.view_cx_conversations: Source table for conversation metadata and tags
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer of this data

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-10-23
OWNER: Data Team

NOTE: Despite the "view_" prefix, this is actually a TABLE, not a VIEW. We use the "view_"
prefix to keep it sorted alongside our other view_* objects. This must be a table (not a view)
to avoid DROP CASCADE issues when materialized views are refreshed.
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new
DISTKEY(conversation_id)
SORTKEY(conversation_created_date, part_created_date)
AS
WITH all_parts AS (
  -- Get all comment parts from conversation_parts
  -- Each row represents one message in a conversation
  SELECT
    cp.id AS part_id,
    cp.body,
    cp.author,
    cp.conversation_id,
    cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
    -- Get first created_at from parts for this conversation
    MIN(cp.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY cp.conversation_id) AS conversation_created_at
  FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
  WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
)
SELECT
  part_id,
  body,
  REPLACE(author.type::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_type,
  REPLACE(author.email::VARCHAR, '"', '') AS author_email,
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS conversation_id,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + part_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS part_created_date,
  (TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + conversation_created_at * INTERVAL '1 second')::TIMESTAMP AS conversation_created_date,
  -- Flag for automated bot messages to filter out spam
  CASE
    WHEN body ILIKE '%unmonitored inbox%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS bot_spam,
  -- Flag conversations mentioning 1583, verification, primary, or secondary accounts
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(body) ILIKE '%1583%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%verification%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%primary%'
    OR LOWER(body) ILIKE '%secondary%' THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS mentions_1583
FROM all_parts;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;
ALTER TABLE view_intercom_conversations_with_topics_new RENAME TO view_intercom_conversations_with_topics;

COMMIT;

content.text:
Perfect! I've reverted `08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql` to be completely independent. The key changes:

## What Changed:

1. **Removed all dependencies** on `view_cx_conversations` and `intercom_conversations`
2. **Simplified the CTE** - now only reads from `intercom_conversation_parts`
3. **Removed tag checking** from the mentions_1583 logic (tags are conversation-level, not message-level)
4. **Removed original_source** column (not relevant for message-level data)
5. **Removed the placeholder logic** for conversations without parts (that's a conversation-level concern)

## The view now:
- **Pure message-level analysis** - one row per comment part
- **No conversation aggregation** - that will happen in the enriched view
- **Clean separation of concerns** - only looks at message bodies for topic detection
- **Uses window function** to get conversation_created_at from the earliest part

This clean separation means:
- `07_view_cx_conversations` = conversation facts & tags
- `08_view_intercom_conversations_with_topics` = message-level topic detection  
- `09_view_cx_conversations_enriched` (to be created) = combined view with topic aggregation

Ready to create the enriched view (09) that brings it all together?

input.content:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations_enriched
PURPOSE: Enriched conversation view combining base data with topic analysis
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table combines conversation-level data from view_cx_conversations with 
aggregated topic insights from view_intercom_conversations_with_topics. It provides
a complete view of each conversation including both structured metadata (tags, metrics)
and content-based analysis (topic detection from message bodies).

USE CASES:
- Quality control: Find conversations where message content doesn't match tags
- Topic analysis: Understand prevalence of specific topics across conversations
- Customer insights: See which customers frequently mention certain topics
- Support optimization: Identify conversations that may need tag updates
- Reporting: Complete conversation metrics with topic enrichment

KEY COLUMNS:
All columns from view_cx_conversations plus:
- has_1583_in_tags: TRUE if conversation has 1583-related tags
- has_1583_in_messages: TRUE if any message mentions 1583/verification/primary/secondary
- count_1583_mentions: Number of messages mentioning these topics
- missing_1583_tag: TRUE if messages mention 1583 but conversation lacks the tag
- unexpected_1583_tag: TRUE if conversation has 1583 tag but no mentions in messages
- total_message_parts: Total number of comment parts in the conversation
- non_spam_message_count: Count of messages excluding bot spam

DATA SOURCES:
- view_cx_conversations: Base conversation data with tags and metrics
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis

ENRICHMENT LOGIC:
1. Start with all conversations from view_cx_conversations
2. LEFT JOIN aggregated topic data from message analysis
3. Compare tags vs actual message content for quality insights
4. Note: Hiver conversations won't have message-level data (Intercom only)

QUALITY CONTROL FLAGS:
- missing_1583_tag: Conversations discussing 1583 topics but not tagged
- unexpected_1583_tag: Conversations tagged for 1583 but no actual mentions
These flags help identify tagging improvements and training opportunities

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Aggregation happens at query time for flexibility

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find conversations missing appropriate 1583 tags
SELECT 
  id,
  customer_email,
  created_at,
  count_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE missing_1583_tag = TRUE
  AND original_source = 'Intercom'
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Analyze tag accuracy
SELECT 
  original_source,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_tags THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as tagged_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as mentions_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN missing_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as missing_tags,
  SUM(CASE WHEN unexpected_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as unnecessary_tags
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
GROUP BY 1;

-- Customer topic frequency
SELECT 
  customer_email,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as conversations_about_1583,
  SUM(count_1583_mentions) as total_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE customer_email IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
ORDER BY 3 DESC;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_cx_conversations: Source for conversation data
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Source for topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for enriched analytics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-25
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH topic_aggregates AS (
  -- Aggregate topic data at conversation level
  SELECT
    conversation_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_message_parts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN bot_spam = FALSE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_spam_message_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_1583_mentions,
    MAX(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_1583_in_messages
  FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
  GROUP BY conversation_id
)
SELECT
  -- All base conversation columns
  c.*,
  
  -- Topic enrichment for Intercom conversations
  -- Check if conversation has 1583-related tags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS has_1583_in_tags,
  
  -- Message-level topic flags (NULL for Hiver since no message data)
  COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) AS has_1583_in_messages,
  COALESCE(t.count_1583_mentions, 0) AS count_1583_mentions,
  
  -- Quality control flags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom' 
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = TRUE
      AND NOT (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS missing_1583_tag,
  
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom'
      AND (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = FALSE
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS unexpected_1583_tag,
  
  -- Message counts
  COALESCE(t.total_message_parts, 0) AS total_message_parts,
  COALESCE(t.non_spam_message_count, 0) AS non_spam_message_count
  
FROM view_cx_conversations c
LEFT JOIN topic_aggregates t 
  ON c.id = t.conversation_id::VARCHAR
  AND c.original_source = 'Intercom';  -- Only join for Intercom conversations

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations_enriched;

COMMIT;

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance

USAGE EXAMPLES:

WHERE customer_email IS NOT NULL

RELATED OBJECTS:

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-25
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new

SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH topic_aggregates AS (
  -- Aggregate topic data at conversation level
  SELECT
    conversation_id,

    SUM(CASE WHEN bot_spam = FALSE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_spam_message_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_1583_mentions,
    MAX(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_1583_in_messages
  FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics

SELECT
  -- All base conversation columns
  c.*,
  
  -- Topic enrichment for Intercom conversations
  -- Check if conversation has 1583-related tags

      AND NOT (

        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')

      )

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
