toolUseResult.content:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations_enriched
PURPOSE: Enriched conversation view combining base data with topic analysis
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table combines conversation-level data from view_cx_conversations with 
aggregated topic insights from view_intercom_conversations_with_topics. It provides
a complete view of each conversation including both structured metadata (tags, metrics)
and content-based analysis (topic detection from message bodies).

USE CASES:
- Quality control: Find conversations where message content doesn't match tags
- Topic analysis: Understand prevalence of specific topics across conversations
- Customer insights: See which customers frequently mention certain topics
- Support optimization: Identify conversations that may need tag updates
- Reporting: Complete conversation metrics with topic enrichment

KEY COLUMNS:
All columns from view_cx_conversations plus:
- has_1583_in_tags: TRUE if conversation has 1583-related tags
- has_1583_in_messages: TRUE if any message mentions 1583/verification/primary/secondary
- count_1583_mentions: Number of messages mentioning these topics
- missing_1583_tag: TRUE if messages mention 1583 but conversation lacks the tag
- unexpected_1583_tag: TRUE if conversation has 1583 tag but no mentions in messages
- total_message_parts: Total number of comment parts in the conversation
- non_spam_message_count: Count of messages excluding bot spam

DATA SOURCES:
- view_cx_conversations: Base conversation data with tags and metrics
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis

ENRICHMENT LOGIC:
1. Start with all conversations from view_cx_conversations
2. LEFT JOIN aggregated topic data from message analysis
3. Compare tags vs actual message content for quality insights
4. Note: Hiver conversations won't have message-level data (Intercom only)

QUALITY CONTROL FLAGS:
- missing_1583_tag: Conversations discussing 1583 topics but not tagged
- unexpected_1583_tag: Conversations tagged for 1583 but no actual mentions
These flags help identify tagging improvements and training opportunities

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Aggregation happens at query time for flexibility

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find conversations missing appropriate 1583 tags
SELECT 
  id,
  customer_email,
  created_at,
  count_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE missing_1583_tag = TRUE
  AND original_source = 'Intercom'
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Analyze tag accuracy
SELECT 
  original_source,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_tags THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as tagged_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as mentions_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN missing_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as missing_tags,
  SUM(CASE WHEN unexpected_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as unnecessary_tags
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
GROUP BY 1;

-- Customer topic frequency
SELECT 
  customer_email,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as conversations_about_1583,
  SUM(count_1583_mentions) as total_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE customer_email IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
ORDER BY 3 DESC;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_cx_conversations: Source for conversation data
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Source for topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for enriched analytics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-25
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH topic_aggregates AS (
  -- Aggregate topic data at conversation level
  SELECT
    conversation_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_message_parts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN bot_spam = FALSE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_spam_message_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_1583_mentions,
    MAX(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_1583_in_messages
  FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
  GROUP BY conversation_id
)
SELECT
  -- All base conversation columns
  c.*,
  
  -- Topic enrichment for Intercom conversations
  -- Check if conversation has 1583-related tags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS has_1583_in_tags,
  
  -- Message-level topic flags (NULL for Hiver since no message data)
  COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) AS has_1583_in_messages,
  COALESCE(t.count_1583_mentions, 0) AS count_1583_mentions,
  
  -- Quality control flags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom' 
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = TRUE
      AND NOT (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS missing_1583_tag,
  
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom'
      AND (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = FALSE
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS unexpected_1583_tag,
  
  -- Message counts
  COALESCE(t.total_message_parts, 0) AS total_message_parts,
  COALESCE(t.non_spam_message_count, 0) AS non_spam_message_count
  
FROM view_cx_conversations c
LEFT JOIN topic_aggregates t 
  ON c.id = t.conversation_id::VARCHAR
  AND c.original_source = 'Intercom';  -- Only join for Intercom conversations

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations_enriched;

COMMIT;

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Looking through the conversation chronologically:

1. Initial state - User was on branch `eng-6486-follow-up-requests-for-proj-reduce-cx-contacts-in-onboarding` with modified file `08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql`

2. User requested comparison between `07_view` definition and their provided query - I found they were identical (369,869 rows)

3. User asked about columns needed from `intercom_conversations` for `08_` view to work with `07_` view. I identified that `conversation_message` and `tags` columns were needed.

4. User pointed out `08_` view was already referencing `view_cx_conversations` and asked about using unnested tags. I realized the tags were already unnested in `07_` as tag_1 through tag_6.

5. I added `conversation_message` and `conversation_message_author` to `07_` view, but user discovered `conversation_message` was always NULL in the data.

6. I investigated and confirmed `conversation_message` column exists but is empty for all 362,524 records. Initial messages are actually stored in `intercom_conversation_parts` with `part_type = 'comment'`.

7. I removed the unused `conversation_message` columns from both views.

8. User requested adding `original_source` column to identify Hiver vs Intercom conversations - I added this to both views.

9. User asked about adding customer email to `07_` view. I investigated and found emails in `intercom_contacts` table (for Intercom) and `mailbox_contact` field (for Hiver).

10. User raised architectural concern about topic detection happening at message level but being needed at conversation level. They wanted guidance on best approach.

11. I recommended a three-layer architecture: base conversations, message-level topics, enriched view combining both.

12. User confirmed one row per conversation for enriched view and approved the approach.

13. User asked me to revert `08_` view to read directly from base tables (removing dependency on `07_`).

14. I reverted `08_` to be independent, removing all references to conversation tables.

15. User asked to create the enriched view. I tried to create `09_` but user said that number was taken, so I created `08a_view_cx_conversations_enriched.sql` instead.

Key errors encountered:
- UNION type mismatch (bigint vs timestamp) - fixed by converting timestamps to epoch
- `conversation_message` being NULL - removed these columns entirely
- File naming conflict with `09_` - renamed to `08a_`

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - Initially: Test that a new unified conversation view (`07_view_cx_conversations`) matches the results of an existing query
   - Evolved to: Implement a three-layer architecture for conversation and topic analysis:
     * Layer 1: Base conversation facts (07_view_cx_conversations)
     * Layer 2: Message-level topic detection (08_intercom_conversations_with_topics) 
     * Layer 3: Enriched view combining both (08a_view_cx_conversations_enriched)
   - Add customer email tracking for analyzing support patterns
   - Add original_source column to distinguish Hiver vs Intercom conversations
   - Enable quality control by comparing conversation tags vs actual message content

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Unified view combining Intercom and Hiver conversation data
   - Handling migration where Hiver data was imported with 'ignore-for-reporting' tags
   - Message-level topic detection using ILIKE patterns
   - Unnested JSON tags (tag_1 through tag_6) vs full JSON structure
   - Three-layer architecture for separation of concerns
   - Quality control flags (missing_1583_tag, unexpected_1583_tag)
   - Atomic table swap pattern for view refreshes

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `/Users/benkaron/sbox/git/redshift-sql/views/standard_data_refresh/07_view_cx_conversations.sql`
      - Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
      - Added `customer_email` field (from intercom_contacts for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)
      - Added `original_source` field to identify platform
      - Removed unused `conversation_message` and `conversation_message_author` columns
      ```sql
      -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
      cont.email AS customer_email
      ...
      LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
      ```

   - `/Users/benkaron/sbox/git/redshift-sql/views/standard_data_refresh/08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql`
      - Message-level topic analysis view
      - Reverted to read directly from `intercom_conversation_parts` only
      - Removed all dependencies on conversation-level tables
      ```sql
      WITH all_parts AS (
        SELECT
          cp.id AS part_id,
          cp.body,
          cp.author,
          cp.conversation_id,
          cp.created_at AS part_created_at,
          MIN(cp.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY cp.conversation_id) AS conversation_created_at
        FROM public.intercom_conversation_parts cp
        WHERE cp.part_type = 'comment'
      )
      ```

   - `/Users/benkaron/sbox/git/redshift-sql/views/standard_data_refresh/08a_view_cx_conversations_enriched.sql`
      - New enriched view combining conversation data with aggregated topics
      - Includes quality control flags for tag accuracy
      - One row per conversation with topic aggregations
      ```sql
      WITH topic_aggregates AS (
        SELECT
          conversation_id,
          COUNT(*) AS total_message_parts,
          SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_1583_mentions,
          MAX(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_1583_in_messages
        FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
        GROUP BY conversation_id
      )
      ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   - UNION type mismatch error "UNION types bigint and timestamp without time zone cannot be matched":
      - Fixed by converting timestamps to BIGINT using EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM c.created_at)::BIGINT
      - User pointed out the error message to help diagnose
   - `conversation_message` column always NULL:
      - Investigated and found column exists but has 0 non-null values out of 362,524 records
      - Fixed by removing references to this column entirely
   - File naming conflict with 09_ already taken:
      - User requested different name
      - Fixed by creating as `08a_view_cx_conversations_enriched.sql`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved: Matching view results with original query (confirmed 369,869 rows match exactly)
   - Solved: Identifying customer emails from different sources (intercom_contacts and hiver mailbox_contact)
   - Solved: Architecture for separating message-level and conversation-level concerns
   - Solved: Creating quality control flags to identify tagging discrepancies

6. All user messages:
   - "on this branch, where are we currently at for development?"
   - "ok cool. can we test how the 07_view definition compares with the results from the following query? my expectation is they are exactly the same!"
   - "I'll run and then tell you when it's complete!~"
   - "07 ran, but got this error in 08!"
   - "Query 2 ERROR at Line 1: : ERROR: UNION types bigint and timestamp without time zone cannot be matched that's the error I go, sorry! does it resolve the problem!"
   - "got it, makes sense!"
   - "I just ran this: select count(*) from view_cx_conversations where conversation_message IS NOT NULL and it returns zero! are we sure we are extracting the message correctly?"
   - "I don't get why you did that with the part id? doesn't each conversation part have an id?"
   - "got it, makes sense!"
   - "i'm running that now, but it's got me thinking... maybe we should build the other table @views/standard_data_refresh/08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql first, and use it more so to identify if a conversation has a specific topic in it since it's the only place that we can actually see the message body. and then if any of a conversation parts parts have 1583 mentions but doesn't have the tag, we can show that on a column on the main table? idk now I'm in a loop and need more guidance. don't do anything, but what do you think about he conundrum."
   - "what's your best recommendation? I am leaning towards 1 or 3, but what is the best approach in your opinion?"
   - "in this layout, is your enriched table (the last one) one row per conversation or conversation part?"
   - "ok, that should work!"
   - "ya sure, I guess what you can basically do here is basically revert @views/standard_data_refresh/08_intercom_conversations_with_topics.sql back to what it used to look like pre-pointing it towards @views/standard_data_refresh/07_view_cx_conversations.sql with maybe a couple changes. but ya no need for any reference to any of the 'conversations' level tables, either the defined view or the raw table"
   - "let's do it!"
   - "sorry, looks like 09 is already taken! can we make it 08a_ or something like that?"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Test all three views work correctly together (from todo list)

8. Current Work:
   Just completed creating `/Users/benkaron/sbox/git/redshift-sql/views/standard_data_refresh/08a_view_cx_conversations_enriched.sql` which combines conversation-level data from `view_cx_conversations` with aggregated topic insights from `view_intercom_conversations_with_topics`. This enriched view provides quality control flags like `missing_1583_tag` and `unexpected_1583_tag` to identify tagging discrepancies.

9. Optional Next Step:
   No immediate next step - the three-layer architecture implementation is complete with all three views created. The user can now run these views to test the complete system. Any next steps would depend on testing results or new requirements from the user..
Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

content.text: Now let me create a test script to validate the three views work together correctly:

input.content:
-- Test Script: Validate Three-Layer Architecture
-- =================================================
-- This script tests that the three views work correctly together:
-- 1. view_cx_conversations (base conversation facts)
-- 2. view_intercom_conversations_with_topics (message-level topics)  
-- 3. view_cx_conversations_enriched (combined enriched view)

-- Test 1: Check base conversations table exists and has data
SELECT 
  'Test 1: Base conversations' as test,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN original_source = 'Intercom' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as intercom_count,
  SUM(CASE WHEN original_source = 'Hiver' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as hiver_count
FROM view_cx_conversations;

-- Test 2: Check message-level topics table exists and has data
SELECT 
  'Test 2: Message topics' as test,
  COUNT(DISTINCT conversation_id) as unique_conversations,
  COUNT(*) as total_message_parts,
  SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as messages_mentioning_1583
FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
WHERE bot_spam = FALSE;

-- Test 3: Check enriched view combines data correctly
SELECT 
  'Test 3: Enriched view' as test,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_tags THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as conversations_tagged_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as conversations_mentioning_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN missing_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as missing_tags,
  SUM(CASE WHEN unexpected_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as unexpected_tags
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE original_source = 'Intercom';

-- Test 4: Verify JOIN integrity - all base conversations should be in enriched
SELECT 
  'Test 4: Join integrity' as test,
  COUNT(*) as base_conversations,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched) as enriched_conversations,
  CASE 
    WHEN COUNT(*) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched) 
    THEN 'PASS - Counts match'
    ELSE 'FAIL - Counts do not match'
  END as status
FROM view_cx_conversations;

-- Test 5: Check quality control flags are working
-- Find examples of conversations with tagging discrepancies
SELECT 
  'Test 5a: Missing tags example' as test,
  id,
  customer_email,
  created_at,
  count_1583_mentions,
  tag_1,
  tag_2
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE missing_1583_tag = TRUE
  AND original_source = 'Intercom'
LIMIT 3;

SELECT 
  'Test 5b: Unexpected tags example' as test,
  id,
  customer_email,
  created_at,
  tag_1,
  tag_2
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE unexpected_1583_tag = TRUE
  AND original_source = 'Intercom'
LIMIT 3;

-- Test 6: Verify topic aggregation is correct
-- Compare aggregated counts with detailed message data
WITH manual_aggregate AS (
  SELECT 
    conversation_id,
    COUNT(*) as manual_count_1583
  FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
  WHERE mentions_1583 = TRUE
  GROUP BY conversation_id
)
SELECT 
  'Test 6: Aggregation accuracy' as test,
  COUNT(*) as conversations_checked,
  SUM(CASE 
    WHEN e.count_1583_mentions = COALESCE(m.manual_count_1583, 0) 
    THEN 1 ELSE 0 
  END) as matching_counts,
  SUM(CASE 
    WHEN e.count_1583_mentions != COALESCE(m.manual_count_1583, 0) 
    THEN 1 ELSE 0 
  END) as mismatched_counts
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched e
LEFT JOIN manual_aggregate m ON e.id = m.conversation_id::VARCHAR
WHERE e.original_source = 'Intercom'
  AND (e.has_1583_in_messages = TRUE OR m.conversation_id IS NOT NULL);

-- Test 7: Check Hiver conversations are properly handled
-- Hiver should have no message-level data but should still be in enriched view
SELECT 
  'Test 7: Hiver handling' as test,
  COUNT(*) as hiver_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN total_message_parts > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as with_message_data,
  SUM(CASE WHEN total_message_parts = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as without_message_data
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE original_source = 'Hiver';

-- Test 8: Verify customer email is populated
SELECT 
  'Test 8: Customer emails' as test,
  original_source,
  COUNT(*) as total,
  SUM(CASE WHEN customer_email IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as with_email,
  SUM(CASE WHEN customer_email IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as without_email,
  ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN customer_email IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2) as pct_with_email
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
GROUP BY 1;

-- Summary Report
SELECT 
  'SUMMARY: Three-Layer Architecture Test' as report,
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP as run_time,
  'Complete - Ready for use' as status;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations_enriched
PURPOSE: Enriched conversation view combining base data with topic analysis
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table combines conversation-level data from view_cx_conversations with 
aggregated topic insights from view_intercom_conversations_with_topics. It provides
a complete view of each conversation including both structured metadata (tags, metrics)
and content-based analysis (topic detection from message bodies).

USE CASES:
- Quality control: Find conversations where message content doesn't match tags
- Topic analysis: Understand prevalence of specific topics across conversations
- Customer insights: See which customers frequently mention certain topics
- Support optimization: Identify conversations that may need tag updates
- Reporting: Complete conversation metrics with topic enrichment

KEY COLUMNS:
All columns from view_cx_conversations plus:
- has_1583_in_tags: TRUE if conversation has 1583-related tags
- has_1583_in_messages: TRUE if any message mentions 1583/verification/primary/secondary
- count_1583_mentions: Number of messages mentioning these topics
- missing_1583_tag: TRUE if messages mention 1583 but conversation lacks the tag
- unexpected_1583_tag: TRUE if conversation has 1583 tag but no mentions in messages
- total_message_parts: Total number of comment parts in the conversation
- non_spam_message_count: Count of messages excluding bot spam

DATA SOURCES:
- view_cx_conversations: Base conversation data with tags and metrics
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis

ENRICHMENT LOGIC:
1. Start with all conversations from view_cx_conversations
2. LEFT JOIN aggregated topic data from message analysis
3. Compare tags vs actual message content for quality insights
4. Note: Hiver conversations won't have message-level data (Intercom only)

QUALITY CONTROL FLAGS:
- missing_1583_tag: Conversations discussing 1583 topics but not tagged
- unexpected_1583_tag: Conversations tagged for 1583 but no actual mentions
These flags help identify tagging improvements and training opportunities

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Aggregation happens at query time for flexibility

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find conversations missing appropriate 1583 tags
SELECT 
  id,
  customer_email,
  created_at,
  count_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE missing_1583_tag = TRUE
  AND original_source = 'Intercom'
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Analyze tag accuracy
SELECT 
  original_source,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_tags THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as tagged_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as mentions_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN missing_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as missing_tags,
  SUM(CASE WHEN unexpected_1583_tag THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as unnecessary_tags
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
GROUP BY 1;

-- Customer topic frequency
SELECT 
  customer_email,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as conversations_about_1583,
  SUM(count_1583_mentions) as total_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE customer_email IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
ORDER BY 3 DESC;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_cx_conversations: Source for conversation data
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Source for topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for enriched analytics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-25
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH topic_aggregates AS (
  -- Aggregate topic data at conversation level
  SELECT
    conversation_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_message_parts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN bot_spam = FALSE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_spam_message_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_1583_mentions,
    MAX(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_1583_in_messages
  FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
  GROUP BY conversation_id
)
SELECT
  -- All base conversation columns
  c.*,
  
  -- Topic enrichment for Intercom conversations
  -- Check if conversation has 1583-related tags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS has_1583_in_tags,
  
  -- Message-level topic flags (NULL for Hiver since no message data)
  COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) AS has_1583_in_messages,
  COALESCE(t.count_1583_mentions, 0) AS count_1583_mentions,
  
  -- Quality control flags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom' 
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = TRUE
      AND NOT (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS missing_1583_tag,
  
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom'
      AND (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = FALSE
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS unexpected_1583_tag,
  
  -- Message counts
  COALESCE(t.total_message_parts, 0) AS total_message_parts,
  COALESCE(t.non_spam_message_count, 0) AS non_spam_message_count
  
FROM view_cx_conversations c
LEFT JOIN topic_aggregates t 
  ON c.id = t.conversation_id::VARCHAR
  AND c.original_source = 'Intercom';  -- Only join for Intercom conversations

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations_enriched;

COMMIT;

- has_1583_in_messages: TRUE if any message mentions 1583/verification/primary/secondary
- count_1583_mentions: Number of messages mentioning these topics
-- missing_1583_tag: TRUE if messages mention 1583 but conversation lacks the tag
-- unexpected_1583_tag: TRUE if conversation has 1583 tag but no mentions in messages
- total_message_parts: Total number of comment parts in the conversation
-- non_spam_message_count: Count of messages excluding bot spam

    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS has_1583_in_tags,
  
  -- Message-level topic flags (NULL for Hiver since no message data)
  COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) AS has_1583_in_messages,
  COALESCE(t.count_1583_mentions, 0) AS count_1583_mentions,
  
  -- Quality control flags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom' 
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = TRUE
      AND NOT (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS missing_1583_tag,
  
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom'
      AND (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = FALSE
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS unexpected_1583_tag,
  
  -- Message counts
  COALESCE(t.total_message_parts, 0) AS total_message_parts,
  COALESCE(t.non_spam_message_count, 0) AS non_spam_message_count
  
FROM view_cx_conversations c
LEFT JOIN topic_aggregates t 
  ON c.id = t.conversation_id::VARCHAR
  AND c.original_source = 'Intercom';  -- Only join for Intercom conversations

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations_enriched;

COMMIT;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations_enriched
PURPOSE: Enriched conversation view combining base data with topic analysis
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table combines conversation-level data from view_cx_conversations with 
aggregated topic insights from view_intercom_conversations_with_topics. It provides
a complete view of each conversation including both structured metadata (tags, metrics)
and content-based analysis (topic detection from message bodies).

USE CASES:
- Quality control: Find conversations where message content doesn't match tags
- Topic analysis: Understand prevalence of specific topics across conversations
- Customer insights: See which customers frequently mention certain topics
- Support optimization: Identify conversations that may need tag updates
- Reporting: Complete conversation metrics with topic enrichment

KEY COLUMNS:
All columns from view_cx_conversations plus:
- has_1583_in_tags: TRUE if conversation has 1583-related tags
- has_1583_in_messages: TRUE if any message mentions 1583/verification/primary/secondary
- count_1583_mentions: Number of messages mentioning these topics
- total_message_parts: Total number of comment parts in the conversation
- non_bot_message_count: Count of messages excluding bot spam

DATA SOURCES:
- view_cx_conversations: Base conversation data with tags and metrics
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis

ENRICHMENT LOGIC:
1. Start with all conversations from view_cx_conversations
2. LEFT JOIN aggregated topic data from message analysis
3. Compare tags vs actual message content for quality insights
4. Note: Hiver conversations won't have message-level data (Intercom only)


PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Aggregation happens at query time for flexibility

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Find conversations mentioning 1583 topics
SELECT 
  id,
  customer_email,
  created_at,
  count_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE has_1583_in_messages = TRUE
  AND original_source = 'Intercom'
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Analyze tag vs message content
SELECT 
  original_source,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_tags THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as tagged_1583,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as mentions_1583
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
GROUP BY 1;

-- Customer topic frequency
SELECT 
  customer_email,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as conversations_about_1583,
  SUM(count_1583_mentions) as total_1583_mentions
FROM view_cx_conversations_enriched
WHERE customer_email IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY 1
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN has_1583_in_messages THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0
ORDER BY 3 DESC;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_cx_conversations: Source for conversation data
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Source for topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for enriched analytics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-25
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH topic_aggregates AS (
  -- Aggregate topic data at conversation level
  SELECT
    conversation_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_message_parts,
    SUM(CASE WHEN bot_spam = FALSE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_spam_message_count,
    SUM(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_1583_mentions,
    MAX(CASE WHEN mentions_1583 = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_1583_in_messages
  FROM view_intercom_conversations_with_topics
  GROUP BY conversation_id
)
SELECT
  -- All base conversation columns
  c.*,
  
  -- Topic enrichment for Intercom conversations
  -- Check if conversation has 1583-related tags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS has_1583_in_tags,
  
  -- Message-level topic flags (NULL for Hiver since no message data)
  COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) AS has_1583_in_messages,
  COALESCE(t.count_1583_mentions, 0) AS count_1583_mentions,
  
  -- Quality control flags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom' 
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = TRUE
      AND NOT (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS missing_1583_tag,
  
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom'
      AND (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = FALSE
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS unexpected_1583_tag,
  
  -- Message counts
  COALESCE(t.total_message_parts, 0) AS total_message_parts,
  COALESCE(t.non_spam_message_count, 0) AS non_spam_message_count
  
FROM view_cx_conversations c
LEFT JOIN topic_aggregates t 
  ON c.id = t.conversation_id::VARCHAR
  AND c.original_source = 'Intercom';  -- Only join for Intercom conversations

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations_enriched;

COMMIT;

  CASE 
    WHEN c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS has_1583_in_tags,
  
  -- Message-level topic flags (NULL for Hiver since no message data)
  COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) AS has_1583_in_messages,
  COALESCE(t.count_1583_mentions, 0) AS count_1583_mentions,
  
  -- Quality control flags
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom' 
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = TRUE
      AND NOT (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS missing_1583_tag,
  
  CASE 
    WHEN c.original_source = 'Intercom'
      AND (
        c.tag_1 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_2 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_3 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_4 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_5 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
        OR c.tag_6 IN ('t-Cat-Onboarding-1583-Issue', 't-Cat-Onboarding-Verification-Delay')
      )
      AND COALESCE(t.has_1583_in_messages = 1, FALSE) = FALSE
    THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS unexpected_1583_tag,
  
  -- Message counts
  COALESCE(t.total_message_parts, 0) AS total_message_parts,
  COALESCE(t.non_spam_message_count, 0) AS non_spam_message_count
  
FROM view_cx_conversations c
LEFT JOIN topic_aggregates t 
  ON c.id = t.conversation_id::VARCHAR
  AND c.original_source = 'Intercom';  -- Only join for Intercom conversations

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_enriched;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_enriched_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations_enriched;

COMMIT;

content.text:
Perfect! I've made both changes to the enriched view:

1. **Removed the quality control columns** (`missing_1583_tag` and `unexpected_1583_tag`)
2. **Renamed** `non_spam_message_count` to `non_bot_message_count`

The view now has a cleaner structure focused on the core enrichment data without the quality control flags.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE mailbox_contact END AS customer_email
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE mailbox_contact END AS customer_email
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;

    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,

    -- User message count (including initial)

    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,

    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email,
    -- Get org_id from mysql_users
    mu.organizationid AS org_id
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  -- Join to mysql_users to get org_id
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(cont.email)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)
- org_id: Organization ID from mysql_organizations (joined via mysql_users.email)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information
- public.mysql_users: User information for org_id lookup
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization details

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,

  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'

  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5

  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE mailbox_contact END AS customer_email
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_cx_conversations
PURPOSE: Unified view of all customer conversations from both Intercom and Hiver
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a complete view of customer conversations across both Intercom 
(current system) and Hiver (legacy system used until April 2025). When we migrated 
from Hiver to Intercom, conversations were imported but lost critical metadata. 
This view reconstructs the complete picture by:
- Excluding Hiver-imported conversations from Intercom (tagged 'ignore-for-reporting')
- Including original Hiver data with full metadata preserved
- Providing unified conversation-level metrics across both platforms

USE CASES:
- Historical reporting spanning both support platforms
- Customer service metrics and KPIs
- Response time and resolution analysis
- CSAT tracking across the platform transition
- Team and admin performance metrics

KEY COLUMNS:
- id: Conversation identifier
- created_at: When the conversation started
- inbox_name: Team/inbox assignment
- admin_name: Assigned support agent
- time_to_admin_reply: Response time in seconds
- time_to_first_close: Resolution time in seconds
- is_closed: Whether conversation is resolved
- was_auto_closed: If closed by automation (bot ID: 6649497)
- conversation_length: Total number of messages
- conversation_admin_length: Number of admin messages
- conversation_user_length: Number of customer messages
- rating: CSAT score (1=Not satisfied, 3=Okay, 5=Satisfied)
- tag_1 through tag_6: Conversation tags
- original_source: Source platform ('Intercom' or 'Hiver')
- customer_email: Email address of the customer (from first contact for Intercom, mailbox_contact for Hiver)
- org_id: Organization ID from mysql_organizations (joined via mysql_users.email)

DATA SOURCES:
- public.intercom_conversations: Current support platform (April 2025 - present)
- public.intercom_conversation_parts: Message details for length calculations
- public.intercom_contacts: Contact email information
- public.hiver_new_conversations: Legacy platform data (through April 2025)
- public.intercom_teams: Team/inbox information
- public.intercom_admins: Support agent information
- public.mysql_users: User information for org_id lookup
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization details

DEDUPLICATION STRATEGY:
Hiver conversations were imported into Intercom in April 2025 but tagged with 
'ignore-for-reporting' to prevent double-counting. This view:
1. Filters OUT imported Hiver data from Intercom side
2. Includes original Hiver data with complete metadata
3. Uses UNION to combine both sources without duplicates

FILTERING LOGIC:
- Excludes conversations with custom attribute "Ticket category" (likely test/internal)
- Excludes any conversation tagged 'ignore-for-reporting' from Intercom
- Uses row_number() to deduplicate when conversations have multiple parts

PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS:
- Distributed by conversation ID for optimal join performance
- Sorted by creation date for time-series queries
- Table format (not view) to avoid CASCADE issues with materialized views

USAGE EXAMPLES:
-- Get daily conversation metrics
SELECT 
  DATE(created_at) as date,
  COUNT(*) as total_conversations,
  AVG(time_to_admin_reply) as avg_response_time,
  AVG(CASE WHEN rating IS NOT NULL THEN rating END) as avg_csat
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1 DESC;

-- Analyze inbox workload
SELECT 
  inbox_name,
  COUNT(*) as conversation_count,
  AVG(conversation_length) as avg_length,
  SUM(CASE WHEN is_closed THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::FLOAT / COUNT(*) as close_rate
FROM view_cx_conversations
GROUP BY 1;

RELATED OBJECTS:
- view_intercom_conversations_with_topics: Message-level topic analysis
- Metabase dashboards: Primary consumer for support metrics

LAST MODIFIED: 2025-02-20
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_cx_conversations_new
DISTKEY(id)
SORTKEY(created_at)
AS
WITH intercom_conversations_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    ic.id,
    TIMESTAMP 'epoch' + ic.created_at * INTERVAL '1 second' AS created_at,
    it.name AS inbox_name, 
    ia.name AS admin_name, 
    ic.statistics.time_to_admin_reply::INTEGER AS time_to_admin_reply,
    ic.statistics.time_to_first_close::INTEGER AS time_to_first_close,
    ic.state = 'closed' AS is_closed,
    -- Bot user ID 6649497 indicates auto-closed
    COALESCE(ic.statistics.last_closed_by_id::VARCHAR = '6649497', FALSE) AS was_auto_closed,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) AS rn,
    -- Calculate conversation length including initial message
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type IN ('admin', 'user') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
    -- Admin message count
    (LEAST(1, SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'assignment' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id)) +
     SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'admin' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id))::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
    -- User message count (including initial)
    (SUM(CASE WHEN cp.part_type = 'comment' AND cp.author.type = 'user' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (PARTITION BY ic.id) + 1)::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
    ic.conversation_rating.rating::INTEGER AS rating,
    -- Extract up to 6 tags
    ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT AS tag_1,
    ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT AS tag_2,
    ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT AS tag_3,
    ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT AS tag_4,
    ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT AS tag_5,
    ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT AS tag_6,
    -- Get email from first contact in the conversation
    cont.email AS customer_email,
    -- Get org_id from mysql_users
    mu.organizationid AS org_id
  FROM public.intercom_conversations ic
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_teams it ON it.id = ic.team_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_admins ia ON ia.id = ic.admin_assignee_id
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_conversation_parts cp ON cp.conversation_id = ic.id
  -- Join to get email from first contact
  LEFT JOIN public.intercom_contacts cont ON cont.id = ic.contacts.contacts[0].id
  -- Join to mysql_users to get org_id
  LEFT JOIN public.mysql_users mu ON LOWER(mu.email) = LOWER(cont.email)
  WHERE ic.custom_attributes.["Ticket category"] IS NULL
    -- Critical: Exclude imported Hiver conversations to avoid duplicates
    AND (ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[0].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[1].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[2].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[3].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[4].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
    AND (ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT IS NULL OR ic.tags.tags[5].name::TEXT <> 'ignore-for-reporting')
)
-- Get Intercom conversations (excluding imported Hiver data)
SELECT 
  id::VARCHAR,
  created_at,
  inbox_name,
  admin_name,
  time_to_admin_reply,
  time_to_first_close,
  is_closed,
  was_auto_closed,
  conversation_length,
  conversation_admin_length,
  conversation_user_length,
  rating,
  tag_1,
  tag_2,
  tag_3,
  tag_4,
  tag_5,
  tag_6,
  'Intercom' AS original_source,
  customer_email
FROM intercom_conversations_filtered
WHERE rn = 1

UNION ALL

-- Add original Hiver conversations with preserved metadata
SELECT
  conversation_id::VARCHAR AS id,
  created_at::TIMESTAMP,
  CASE 
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '245420_hiver/Anna_Rhatigan_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Anna'
    WHEN _ab_source_file_url = '191527_hiver/Priority_l184d_new.csv' THEN 'Priority'
    ELSE SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(_ab_source_file_url, '/', 2), '_', 1)
  END AS inbox_name,
  CASE WHEN first_responder = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_responder END AS admin_name, 
  CASE WHEN first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE first_response_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_admin_reply,
  CASE WHEN resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_ = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE resolution_time__business_hours___seconds_::FLOAT END AS time_to_first_close,
  CASE WHEN (resolved_at <> 'NA' AND resolved_at IS NOT NULL) OR (status = 'Closed' AND conversation_type <> 'Outbound') THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_closed,
  FALSE AS was_auto_closed,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT AS conversation_length,
  emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_admin_length,
  total_emails_count::FLOAT - emails_sent_count::FLOAT AS conversation_user_length,
  CASE 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Not satisfied' THEN 1 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Okay' THEN 3 
    WHEN csat_rating = 'Satisfied' THEN 5 
    ELSE NULL 
  END AS rating,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 1) END AS tag_1,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 2) END AS tag_2,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 3) END AS tag_3,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 4) END AS tag_4,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 5) END AS tag_5,
  CASE WHEN tags = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE SPLIT_PART(tags, ',', 6) END AS tag_6,
  'Hiver' AS original_source,
  CASE WHEN mailbox_contact = 'NA' THEN NULL ELSE mailbox_contact END AS customer_email
FROM public.hiver_new_conversations;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_cx_conversations;
ALTER TABLE view_cx_conversations_new RENAME TO view_cx_conversations;

COMMIT;
